title: Umami Gooey Jamaican Sweetcorn Stir‑fry
description: A silky, umami‑rich Jamaican sweetcorn stir‑fry that melts in your mouth and is perfect for a cozy date night. It’s simple, kosher‑friendly, and packed with flavor.
instructions: Heat the coconut oil in a large skillet or wok over medium‑high heat until shimmering.
Add the sliced red onion and cook for 1‑2 minutes until it starts to soften and become translucent.
Stir in the minced garlic, ginger, smoked paprika, and cumin; cook for another 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the fresh corn kernels and julienned red bell pepper; toss to combine and let the vegetables cook for 3‑4 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Whisk together the tamari, maple syrup, and miso paste in a small bowl until the miso is fully dissolved.
Pour the sauce over the stir‑fry, season with kosher salt and black pepper, and cook for another 2‑3 minutes, letting the sauce thicken into a glossy, gooey coating.
Remove from heat, squeeze lime juice over the dish, and sprinkle with fresh cilantro if desired.
Serve immediately over a bed of steamed jasmine rice or cauliflower rice for a low‑carb option.
Enjoy the sweet, savory, and slightly tangy flavors that make this a standout date‑night Jamaican dish.
